free_text: Application of non-monoenergetic sources in fast
neutron scattering measurements
Data from experiment, which made in Germany are
included in 22409 and 22412 EXFOR Entry.
The HI-13 Tandem Accelerator in China
Institute of Atomic Energy
From the measured raw spectra (gas in,
gas out, sample in, sample out), the net TOF spectra
are determined, including the propagation of
uncertainties.
Deuterium gas target had following parameters:
Length 30 mm, diam. 11 mm, on gold backing 0.5 mm.
Three main detectors ST-451(10.5 x 5.0 cm)
and one monitor ST-451 (10.5 x 5.0 cm) were used.
The measurement were repeated in abnormal and normal
geometry of spectrometer.
Elemental vanadium, hollow cylinder, outer diam. 35mm,
inner diam. 10.0 mm, height 35.0 mm.
Monte-Carlo simulated TOF spectra of the complete
angular distribution were iteratively adjusted to
the measured TOF spectra. The simulation comprised
the neutron production in the gas target and the
scattering geometry between target, sample and
detectors.
The uncertainties due to the statistical
uncertainty, the neutron detection efficiency,
the scattering angle, the normalization the correction
for  multiple scattering have been taken into
account, including their correlation.
Data were received from Dr. Xichao Ruan
Entry was approved by Dr. Xichao Ruan
SD
SD: data for E-MAX in some cases in
subent 004 were corrected.

were corrected.
